tooltips.auto refill ammo:
Controls the ammo refill behavior when you enter the outfitter.

- "always": ammo will be automatically refilled if you have the credits for it.

- "when free": ammo will be automatically refilled only if it doesn't cost you anything, such as when you have spare ammo in cargo or storage.

- "ask": you will be prompted to refill any missing ammunition upon entering the outfitter.

- "never": you will always have to manually refill your ammunition.
tooltips.title bar theme: Choose the theme of the title bar, or leave "system default" to use system settings. (Available on Windows 10 or newer; on Windows 10, the game window must be resized or restarted in order for the change to take effect.)
tooltips.window rounding: Control the appearance of window corners. (Available on Windows 11.)
tooltips.tribute confirmation:
Whether a confirmation dialog will be displayed when you attempt to demand tribute from a planet.

- "off": you will never be asked for confirmation.

- "friendly only": you will only be asked for confirmation against planets that you are friendly toward.

- "always": you will always be asked for confirmation.
tooltips.minimum value: The lowest value that an outfit or ship can depreciate to relative to its full price. All plundered, captured, or gifted items start at this value, and all purchased items start at 100% value and then depreciate toward this value according to the other depreciation gamerules.
tooltips.grace period: The number of days that must pass before a newly purchased outfit or ship will begin to depreciate. This allows you to have a short amount of time to test out an item and then sell it back at full value if you decide that you do not like it.
tooltips.maximum age: The number of days that must pass, after the grace period has ended, in order for an outfit or ship to become fully depreciated.
tooltips.daily depreciation: The percentage of an item's depreciable value that it maintains day over day. Only impacts the exponential term of the depreciation calculation. If set to 100%, an item will still linearly lose value.
tooltips.spawn attempt period: The number of frames on average that the game waits before attempting to spawn a person ship. The game rolls a random number between 0 and this value minus one every frame. (There are 60 frames in a second.) If the value lands on 0, a person ship spawn attempt is made. In other words, higher values result in lower spawn rates of person ships.
tooltips.no spawn weight: When a person ship spawn attempt is made, this is the "weight" of the chance of having the spawn attempt fail. Each person ship has a "weight" associated with it which determines its chance of spawning. The chance of any single person ship spawning is its weight divided by the sum of the weights of all person ships and this value. That means that the chance of a spawn attempt failing is this number over that same sum.
tooltips.npc max mining time: Randomly spawned NPCs with the "mining" personality will spend this many frames mining in the current system before deciding to do something else. The purpose of this limit is so that NPC ships can be seen by the player to show how to mine without stripping the system bare of minable asteroids. This limit does not apply to mining ships that are spawned by a mission.
tooltips.universal frugal threshold: Ships with the "frugal" personality will only use weapons that consume ammo or fuel while their health (shields + hull - the hull level when they become disabled) is at or below this percentage, or if the hostiles in the system are considerably stronger than them.
tooltips.universal ramscoop: Determines whether the "universal ramscoop" is active on all ships. This ramscoop provides a very small amount of fuel to every ship regardless of whether it has an actual ramscoop installed. The strength of this ramscoop falls off much more strongly than normal ramscoops, requiring you to be as close to a star as possible to have any real gain in fuel. Having this enabled prevents needing to reload your save file if you run out of fuel and no NPCs spawn in the system that you can get fuel from.
tooltips.minimum arrival distance: The minimum arrival distance for all systems. Applies to both hyperdrive and jump drive travel. If the system has a defined arrival distance greater than this value, then it will be used instead. If this gamerule has a value of "(unset)", then the value defined by the system will always be used.
tooltips.minimum departure distance: The minimum departure distance for all systems. A ship can only jump out of a system if it is farther from the system center than the departure distance. If a system has a defined departure distance greater than this value, then it will be used instead.
tooltips.fleet multiplier: A global fleet spawn rate multiplier for random fleet spawns within systems.
tooltips.lock gamerules: Controls whether gamerules can be altered after a pilot has been created. If true, the gamerules button will not appear on the main menu for this pilot. If you set this to true on an existing pilot, you will not be able to return to this menu after leaving.
tooltips.fighter crossfire immunity:
Controls which carried ships (fighters and drones), when disabled, will not be hit by projectiles unless they are directly targeted. Fighters and drones are fragile ships that can be difficult and/or tedious to replace for the player, so this gamerule is a means of increasing their survivability without increasing their combat effectiveness. Disabled carried ships can still be hit by explosions, whether from weapons or exploding ships.

- "none": no carried ships are affected.

- "player": only ships owned by the player are affected.

- "all": all carried ships are affected.
tooltips.universal ammo stocking: Controls whether the ammo for most secondary weapons that you have installed or in your cargo will be available for purchase at any outfitter, even if that outfitter does not normally sell ammo of that type. The description of each secondary weapon will indicate whether it can be restocked anywhere when this rule is true.
tooltips.spawn raid fleets: Controls whether raid fleets are able to spawn. Raid fleets are hostile fleets that can spawn in some systems if your fleet's cargo attractiveness is much higher than its deterrence. (That is, if you have a lot of cargo space and not a lot of firepower to defend it.) If this rule is active, your fleet's raid fleet spawn chance can be viewed from the player info panel.
tooltips.fleet size limitation:
Determines whether the player can only have a limited number of escorts active at once. If active, your flagship, parked ships, and carried ships (fighters and drones) are excluded from your fleet capacity, and you will not be able to capture ships that would put you over your capacity or take off from a planet if your capacity has been reached.

- "none": you can have as many escorts as you want.

- "ship cap": you can only command up to a certain number of escorts.

- "crew cap": the base crew requirement of your active escorts may not exceed a certain limit.

- "admin cap": the limit is tied to an "administrative cost" that every ship has, with larger ships having a higher cost.
tooltips.default max escort count: If the fleet size limitation is "ship cap," then this is the default maximum number of escorts that you can have active at once. This value may be increased through story events. Changing this value on an existing pilot will have no effect.
tooltips.default max escort crew: If the fleet size limitation is "crew cap," then this is the default maximum sum of the base required crew (i.e. excluding required crew added by outfits like turrets) across all your escorts that you can have at once. Automated ships will use a crew-equivalent value. This value may be increased through story events. Changing this value on an existing pilot will have no effect.

booleanAttributes.installable: This is not an installable item.
booleanAttributes.jump drive: Lets you jump to any nearby system.
booleanAttributes.minable: This item is mined from asteroids.
booleanAttributes.atrocity: This outfit is considered an atrocity.
booleanAttributes.cloaked afterburner: You may use afterburners while cloaked.
booleanAttributes.cloaked communication: You may make hails while cloaked.
booleanAttributes.cloaked deployment: You may deploy from bays while cloaked.
booleanAttributes.cloaked pickup: You may pick up flotsam while cloaked.
booleanAttributes.cloaked scanning: You may scan other ships while cloaked.

damageTypeDetails.description: Adds [burn] heat per frame (DoT). Cut to 50% when shields are up. Decays 1%/frame plus up to [burn resistance] per frame.
damageTypeDetails.applyFormula:
dose = [burn damage] * (1 - [burn protection])
statusEffects.burn += dose
// Per-frame decay (Ship.cpp DoStatusEffect):
statusEffects.burn = max(0, 0.99 * statusEffects.burn - min([burn resistance], 0.99 * statusEffects.burn))

damageTypeDetails.description: Drains hull by [corrosion] per frame (DoT). Ignored entirely when shields are up. Decays 1%/frame plus up to [corrosion resistance] per frame.
damageTypeDetails.applyFormula:
dose = [corrosion damage] * (1 - [corrosion protection])
statusEffects.corrosion += dose
// Per-frame decay (Ship.cpp DoStatusEffect):
statusEffects.corrosion = max(0, 0.99 * statusEffects.corrosion - min([corrosion resistance], 0.99 * statusEffects.corrosion))

damageTypeDetails.description: Drains shields by [discharge] per frame (DoT). Always full effect regardless of shields. Decays 1%/frame plus up to [discharge resistance] per frame.
damageTypeDetails.applyFormula:
dose = [discharge damage] * (1 - [discharge protection])
statusEffects.discharge += dose
// Per-frame decay (Ship.cpp DoStatusEffect):
statusEffects.discharge = max(0, 0.99 * statusEffects.discharge - min([discharge resistance], 0.99 * statusEffects.discharge))

damageTypeDetails.description: NOT HP damage. Multiplies shield damage received: shieldDmg *= (1 + disruption * 0.01). Decays 1%/frame plus up to [disruption resistance] per frame.
damageTypeDetails.applyFormula:
dose = [disruption damage] * (1 - [disruption protection])
statusEffects.disruption += dose
// Per-frame decay (Ship.cpp DoStatusEffect):
statusEffects.disruption = max(0, 0.99 * statusEffects.disruption - min([disruption resistance], 0.99 * statusEffects.disruption))

damageTypeDetails.description: Instantly drains energy. Cut to 50% when shields are up. Protected by [energy protection].
Instantly drains fuel. Cut to 50% when shields are up. Protected by [fuel protection].
Instantly drains heat. Cut to 50% when shields are up. Protected by [heat protection].
Instantly drains hitforce. Protected by [hitforce protection].
Directly reduces hull HP. Protected by [hull protection].
damageTypeDetails.applyFormula:
effectivePiercing = clamp(piercing, 0, 1) * (1 - [piercing resistance])
rawDmg = ([hull damage] + [% hull damage] * currentHull) * (1 - [hull protection])
if shields > 0: hull -= rawDmg * effectivePiercing
else:           hull -= rawDmg

damageTypeDetails.description: Accumulates when hit by ion weapons. Ionization > energy prevents movement-energy weapons from firing (IsIonized). Decays 1%/frame plus up to [ion resistance] per frame.
damageTypeDetails.applyFormula:
dose = [ion damage] * (1 - [ion protection])
statusEffects.ionization += dose
// Per-frame decay (Ship.cpp DoStatusEffect):
statusEffects.ionization = max(0, 0.99 * statusEffects.ionization - min([ion resistance], 0.99 * statusEffects.ionization))

damageTypeDetails.description: Drains fuel by [leak] per frame (DoT). Ignored entirely when shields are up. Decays 1%/frame plus up to [leak resistance] per frame.
damageTypeDetails.applyFormula:
dose = [leak damage] * (1 - [leak protection])
statusEffects.leak += dose
// Per-frame decay (Ship.cpp DoStatusEffect):
statusEffects.leak = max(0, 0.99 * statusEffects.leak - min([leak resistance], 0.99 * statusEffects.leak))

damageTypeDetails.description: Instantly drains scaling. Protected by [scaling protection].
Accumulates when hit by scrambling weapons. Causes weapons to jam: scrambling > 0.1 ? 1 - pow(2, -scrambling/70) : 0. Decays 1%/frame plus up to [scramble resistance] per frame.
Directly reduces shield HP. Protected by [shield protection].
damageTypeDetails.applyFormula:
effectivePiercing = clamp(piercing, 0, 1) * (1 - [piercing resistance])
disruptMult = 1 + statusEffects.disruption * 0.01
rawDmg = ([shield damage] + [% shield damage] * currentShields) * (1 - [shield protection]) * disruptMult
if shields > 0:
    shields -= rawDmg * (1 - effectivePiercing)
    hull    -= rawDmg * effectivePiercing
    if shields < 0: hull += shields * bleedFraction * (1 - [hull protection]); shields = 0
else:
    hull -= [shield damage] + [% shield damage] * maxShields * (1 - [shield protection])

damageTypeDetails.description: NOT HP damage. Reduces thrust and turn rate: speed *= 1/(1 + slowing*0.05). Decays 1%/frame plus up to [slowing resistance] per frame.
damageTypeDetails.applyFormula:
dose = [slowing damage] * (1 - [slowing protection])
statusEffects.slowing += dose
// Per-frame decay (Ship.cpp DoStatusEffect):
statusEffects.slowing = max(0, 0.99 * statusEffects.slowing - min([slowing resistance], 0.99 * statusEffects.slowing))

descriptors.label: Ion
descriptors.description: Accumulates when hit by ion weapons. Ionization > energy prevents movement-energy weapons from firing (IsIonized). Decays 1%/frame plus up to [ion resistance] per frame.
descriptors.decayFormula: stat = max(0, 0.99 * stat - min([ion resistance], 0.99 * stat))

descriptors.label: Scrambling
descriptors.description: Accumulates when hit by scrambling weapons. Causes weapons to jam: scrambling > 0.1 ? 1 - pow(2, -scrambling/70) : 0. Decays 1%/frame plus up to [scramble resistance] per frame.
descriptors.decayFormula: stat = max(0, 0.99 * stat - min([scramble resistance], 0.99 * stat))

descriptors.label: Disruption
descriptors.description: NOT HP damage. Multiplies shield damage received: shieldDmg *= (1 + disruption * 0.01). Decays 1%/frame plus up to [disruption resistance] per frame.
descriptors.decayFormula: stat = max(0, 0.99 * stat - min([disruption resistance], 0.99 * stat))

descriptors.label: Slowing
descriptors.description: NOT HP damage. Reduces thrust and turn rate: speed *= 1/(1 + slowing*0.05). Decays 1%/frame plus up to [slowing resistance] per frame.
descriptors.decayFormula: stat = max(0, 0.99 * stat - min([slowing resistance], 0.99 * stat))

descriptors.label: Discharge
descriptors.description: Drains shields by [discharge] per frame (DoT). Always full effect regardless of shields. Decays 1%/frame plus up to [discharge resistance] per frame.
descriptors.decayFormula: stat = max(0, 0.99 * stat - min([discharge resistance], 0.99 * stat))

descriptors.label: Corrosion
descriptors.description: Drains hull by [corrosion] per frame (DoT). Ignored entirely when shields are up. Decays 1%/frame plus up to [corrosion resistance] per frame.
descriptors.decayFormula: stat = max(0, 0.99 * stat - min([corrosion resistance], 0.99 * stat))

descriptors.label: Burn
descriptors.description: Adds [burn] heat per frame (DoT). Cut to 50% when shields are up. Decays 1%/frame plus up to [burn resistance] per frame.
descriptors.decayFormula: stat = max(0, 0.99 * stat - min([burn resistance], 0.99 * stat))

descriptors.label: Leak
descriptors.description: Drains fuel by [leak] per frame (DoT). Ignored entirely when shields are up. Decays 1%/frame plus up to [leak resistance] per frame.
descriptors.decayFormula: stat = max(0, 0.99 * stat - min([leak resistance], 0.99 * stat))

notes: Passive decay: stat = max(0, 0.99 * stat) — 1%/frame regardless of resistance.
With resistance R: stat = max(0, 0.99*stat - min(R, 0.99*stat)) each frame.
Protection reduces incoming dose: effectiveDose = rawDose * (1 - protection).
Slowing and Disruption are status multipliers, NOT HP damage.
